author: @PaulOvery001
text: Don't you hate it when you paint the wrong side :)

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Hahahaha yes! I didn't realize until I went to put it together lol! Cheers mate! :)

author: @NorthernKitty
text: What a fun project!!!  When you finish, I'll have to fly over to spot you a game of Robotron... I was pro "back in the day", LOL!!  😋

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Omg what a classic I completely forgot about! I would spot a game of MK it's my personal favorite! Thank you! :)

author: @stevetobias4890
text: Cool Luke, will wait to see what you do for the electronics. I personally would have used plywood instead of chipboard because it would be lighter without compromising on strength.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Great suggestion! I'm going to have to alter the control board slightly, maybe I will redo it in ply? I used chipboard because we had 6 massive sheets of it. Thank you mate! :)

author: @1upPaintingAB
text:
I started and never finished a 5V powered portable micro cabinet. Rasp Pi 3B, usb powered hdmi monitor, and usb powered amplifier.
Love seeing folk's custom cabinet designs.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: That sounds awesome! I'm going to be using a rasp pi 2b v1.1, These microcomputers are amazing! Cheers mate! :)

author: @joncleverley3073
text:
I love all your work and your dad's 
But make sure you wear a very long tie to go with your long baggy sleeves doing this kind of work.
Keep safe mate

author: @swagswap
text: Will it contain a computer to run the games?  Where will the sound come out?

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: It will contain a Raspberry Pi. I also have some nice speakers sourced to go in it as well. Thanks mate! :)

replies.author: @swagswap
replies.text: @MyNameIsLukey Pi's a great choice for this application. Can't wait to see how you decorate the cabinet.
